🟡 Colombia Regenerativa

Case Study
Y
links
https://youtu.be/R0G68ROe3U8
Tags

33314415-10e1-8119-ae0e-f5643b5b5db2, 33314415-10e1-81cc-9773-e35aba711f9d, 33314415-10e1-8145-bcae-f4a3e2b81a04, 33314415-10e1-81f9-a733-c2545308b680
background_section
## Background Colombia stands as one of the world's most megadiverse countries, encompassing dramatically varied bioregions that range from Andean mountain systems and high-altitude valleys to Caribbean and Pacific coastlines, dense Amazon rainforest, and vast eastern plains. This biological and cultural richness has made Colombia home to extraordinary biodiversity and dozens of Indigenous and Afro-Colombian communities who have stewarded these lands for millennia. Yet this same diversity faces mounting pressure. Decades of deforestation, soil degradation, extractive economies, and climate change impacts have placed immense strain on Colombia's ecosystems and the communities that depend on them. As the country commits to reducing greenhouse gas emissions by 50% by 2030, the need for regenerative approaches that restore both land and communities has never been more urgent. ### Origins and Network Colombia Regenerativa emerged from a [vision to unite bioregions](https://youtu.be/R0G68ROe3U8) across the country and generate impact at a national scale. While each region maintains its unique identity and grassroots connection, the initiative seeks to give impulse to a broader narrative about bioregionalization. <iframe src="https://www.google.com/maps/d/embed?mid=1bvNxdorYfqTFzCwD6ZqPr2B6BcLuv8t5&ehbc=2E312F" width="640" height="480"></iframe> ### Philosophy & Approach The organization bases its work on Eduardo Muñoz's regenerative development model, which centers Mother Earth as the foundation of the bioregion. This model recognizes that personal and spiritual connections flow outward to create governance and regenerative economic systems that operate in cycles within a territory, fundamentally about the connection between territories and people. This grounding in regenerative principles distinguishes Colombia Regenerativa from conventional development approaches, honoring what life itself is revealing about how to thrive in each place.
patterns

Place
Colombia
place
Colombia
activities
Network Building and Learning Exchange|Three Rings Evaluation Process|Virtual Gaia Digital Mapping Platform|Bioregionalization Course Development
description
A network of regions working from the grassroots level to promote ecological restoration and agroecological production, focusing on returning fertility to soil and communities. The initiative collaborates with the Global Network of Regenerative Communities to share knowledge and experiences with other regions worldwide, particularly in Latin America.
vision_legacy_section
## Vision and Legacy Financing regeneration remains the hardest gap: capital exists, but channels rarely reach living landscapes. The team describes a possible **opening**—a way to [“touch the membrane”](https://youtu.be/R0G68ROe3U8) between financial abstraction and concrete bioregional work—so resources can flow toward repair without pretending ecosystems are ordinary assets. Discussion keeps returning to **capacity and governance**: not only technical “ecosystemic assistance,” but investment in how communities assemble, decide, and hold territory—with social, governance, and economic processes aligned to planetary limits rather than extractive defaults. Across evaluation, mapping, education, and experiments in finance, Colombia Regenerativa is weaving a vision of **many bioregions** contributing national resilience—where diversity of place becomes a source of wisdom instead of a ledger of loss.
website
https://www.colombiaregenerativa.org/
bioregional_context
Colombia encompasses diverse bioregions from Andean mountains and valleys to Caribbean and Pacific coastlines, Amazon rainforest, and extensive plains. This megadiverse country faces significant challenges from deforestation, soil degradation, and climate change impacts.
category
